test.detail: 328 tests passed across 6 test files in 753ms

nonBlockingIssues.description: CombatSystem.applyStun() and applyTaunt() are implemented and tested but never called from ZoneScene — stun is handled via StatusEffectSystem, taunt aggro-forcing never triggers at runtime. consumeStealthBuff() also not called, making stealth damage apply to every hit during buff duration rather than being one-shot.

Three wiring gaps in ZoneScene integration: (1) VFXManager.applyStatusTint() never called — no visual indicators on status-affected entities, (2) Slow speed multiplier not applied to actual movement code, (3) Poison heal reduction not checked in heal/regen paths.
Duplicate item ID 'a_plate_armor' in bases.ts (lines 46 and 60) with conflicting baseDefense values (25 vs 20). Array.find() returns first, so second is dead code.
Three backward exit target coordinates use targetRow: 119 (border wall, unwalkable). No runtime impact since changeZone() currently ignores target coordinates, but semantically wrong.

Pre-existing procs (thornsHeal, killHealPercent, deathSave) use inline ZoneScene logic while new procs use CombatSystem helpers — inconsistency. thornsHeal doesn't emit PLAYER_HEALTH_CHANGED event unlike killHealPercent.
Coverage include list in vitest.config.ts is hardcoded to 5 files — new systems (StatusEffectSystem, etc.) added by later features won't appear in coverage unless manually added.

appliedUpdates.description: Added StatusEffectSystem to per-zone system lifecycle list in architecture.md
Documented PathfindingSystem binary heap internals (MinHeap, flat-array tracking, octile heuristic) in architecture.md
Documented StatusEffectSystem architecture (pure-logic, 6 effect types, DR mechanics, authoritative for CC) in architecture.md
Documented map exit coordinate convention (targets must be walkable interior tiles, not border walls) in architecture.md

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: CC diminishing returns are authoritatively handled by StatusEffectSystem. CombatSystem.applyStun() is dead code and should not be used. Workers adding CC effects should use StatusEffectSystem.apply().
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: bug-fixes-buffs-stun added CombatSystem.applyStun() with its own DR tracking, but status-effect-system added StatusEffectSystem with separate DR tracking that is actually wired into ZoneScene. Two independent DR implementations exist for the same mechanic.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: All combat proc logic (set bonuses, healing effects) should use CombatSystem helper methods rather than inline calculations in ZoneScene. All healing effects must emit PLAYER_HEALTH_CHANGED event.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: set-bonus-effects created CombatSystem helpers (calcThornsHeal, calcKillHeal, checkDeathSave) but pre-existing ZoneScene code still uses inline Math.floor() calculations. thornsHeal doesn't emit PLAYER_HEALTH_CHANGED while killHealPercent does.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: When adding new testable systems, update vitest.config.ts coverage.include to include the new file. Coverage config is not auto-expanding.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: vitest-setup hardcoded 5 files in coverage.include. Later features (StatusEffectSystem, etc.) have test files but won't appear in coverage reports.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: Test assertion bounds should closely match feature spec values (±25% tolerance). Overly wide bounds (e.g., spec says 15-25 but test uses 8-40) can mask regressions.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: numerical-balance feature spec says ~15-25 kills per level, but tests use 10-35 and 8-40 bounds. Lv5 checkpoint is actually ~13.3 kills which is below spec.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: When a feature description includes game loop wiring (e.g., 'Wire into ZoneScene combat loop'), workers must verify that new methods are actually called from ZoneScene, not just implemented and tested in isolation.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: bug-fixes-buffs-stun implemented applyStun(), applyTaunt(), consumeStealthBuff() but none are called from ZoneScene. The worker marked the feature complete without verifying runtime integration.

rejectedObservations.observation: systems-worker skill 'Write tests FIRST (red)' doesn't apply to test infrastructure setup features
rejectedObservations.reason: Single-instance deviation justified for bootstrap/setup features. Not systemic — the TDD convention is correct for all normal feature work.

rejectedObservations.observation: Worker used (w as any).weaponType in test code against project conventions
rejectedObservations.reason: Single instance in one test file. Already captured as non-blocking issue on the feature review. Not a systemic pattern.
